Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendants City and County of San Francisco and Jose Cisneros' Motion and Motion to Tax Costs.
Department 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: L. Bareng. Reporter: Tina Velasquez, CSR #10072. Telephone: 510-301-2918. Email: velasquezmtina@gmail.com.

Appearances via Zoom Videoconference:
Amy L. Silverstein, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Gajanan Inc., A California Corporation, and Engage with SF Hospitality LLC, a California Limited Liability Company.
Thomas S. Lakritz, Esq. appeared for defendants City and County of San Francisco and Jose Cisneros, Treasurer and Tax Collector of the City and County of San Francisco.
Yvonne Detert, a witness for the plaintiffs, was present but did not testify.

Plaintiffs' sued Defendants in four separate actions, which this Court consolidated. Each complaint seeks refund of San Francisco Hotel Tax Penalties from 2014 pursuant to Government Code section 945.6 and San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code sections 6.15-1 through 6.15-4. Plaintiffs alleged that they overpaid hotel tax penalties.

On March 2, 2018, Plaintiffs personally served Defendants with an Offer to Compromise pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 998 for an $850,000.00 judgment against Defendants. Defendants did not respond and trial began on March 12, 2018. On February 11, 2020 this Court entered a judgment in favor of all Plaintiffs; the total judgment amount is $1,680,834.46.

On February 26, 2020, Plaintiffs filed and served a Memorandum of Costs totaling $68,735.32.

Defendants seek to tax a total of $18,100.83. Additionally, Defendants' Motion to Tax Costs requests that the Court tax $10,818.00 in expert witness fees because Plaintiffs' Offer to Compromise pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 998 was invalid. In the alternative, Defendants request that the Court tax $8,775.00 in expert witness fees because Plaintiffs can only recover costs incurred after the offer was rejected or expired.

Plaintiffs oppose Defendants' Motion to Tax Costs. However, Plaintiffs concede that the $1,449.25 in costs from a related case and $8,945.14 in trial transcripts costs for transcripts not requested by the Court should be taxed.
Opposition to Motion to Tax Costs was filed on April 15, 2020.
Reply to Opposition to Motion to Tax Costs was filed on June 19, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Courts' ruling is as follows:
Timeliness of Defendant's Motion to Tax Costs
Ruling: Due to the government "shelter-in-place" orders and the Chief Justice's emergency orders and the San Francisco Superior Court's Presiding Judge's emergency orders, the court finds that the Defendants' Motion to Tax Costs was timely; defendants have not waived any objections to costs.

Messenger and delivery costs:
Motion to tax $827.20 to deliver courtesy copies to court DENIED
Motion to tax $990.50 for service of discovery documents GRANTED
Motion to tax $84.95 to serve defendant, no proof of service GRANTED
Motion to tax $9.95 to deliver demand for exchange of experts GRANTED
Ruling: Therefore, the court is taxing $1,085.40 in MESSENGER & DELIVERY costs.
(9.95 + 990.50 + 84.95 =1,085.40

Discovery Costs:
Motion to tax $527.87 cost is GRANTED
Motion to tax $1,283.65 of the $4347.27 DENIED,
Motion to tax the REMAINDER OF $3063.62 is GRANTED
Ruling: Therefore, the court is taxing $3,591.49 in DISCOVERY costs.
(527.87 + 3,063.62 = 3591.49)

Transcripts not ordered by the court
Ruling: the court taxes $8,945.14 in costs for trial transcripts not ordered by the court. (Plaintiff conceded these costs should be taxed)

The total taxing of costs is $13,623.03

Taxing of Expert Witness Fees:
Ruling: Plaintiffs' Offer to Compromise was valid; whether or not to tax Expert Witness fees pursuant to CCP 998(d) UNDER SUBMISSION, RULING DUE BY AUGUST 30TH.

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And For Imposition Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS GAJANAN INC., and ENGAGE WITH SF HOSPITALITY LLC'S Motion For Protective Order And For Imposition Of Sanctions is DENIED as moot. According to the parties' October 23, 2017 joint filing, the parties' meet and confer efforts have yielded a resolution of all issues raised by this motion. The Court commends the parties for their efforts and ability to work through the issues raised by this motion. In their October 23 joint filing, the parties seek the Court's intervention in resolving the dispute related to the scheduling of the Defendant's PMK witness. This deposition was noticed by the Plaintiffs and was not the subject of the underlying motion. Accordingly, it is not properly before this Court and the Court declines to issue an advisory ruling. However, the Court encourages the parties to continue their meet and confer efforts in the hopes that they can reach a reasonable resolution of the outstanding dispute and avoid expending resources of their clients and the Court in working out a solution. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim. The order may be submitted in electronic form, in Word (.doc) format and directly to the judge pro tem at noah@dplolaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Noah Lebowitz: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)
